sa_devanagari: न मर्षा शरान्तं यदवन्ति देवा विश्वा इत सप्र्धो अभ्यश्नवाव | जयावेदत्र शतनीथमजिं यत सम्यञ्चा मिथुनावभ्यजाव ||
meaning_en: (Agastya): Penance has not been practised in vain; since the gods protect us, we may indulge all our desires; in this world we may triumph in many conflicts, if we exert ourselves mutually together

sa_devanagari: इमं न सोममन्तितो हर्त्सु पीतमुप बरुवे | यत सीमागश्चक्र्मा तत सु मर्ळतु पुलुकामो हि मर्त्यः ||
meaning_en: (Pupil): I beseech the Soma, which has been drunk in my heart, that it may fully expiate the sin we have committed; man is subject to many desires.

sa_devanagari: तं तवा वयं पितो वचोभिर्गावो न हव्या सुषूदिम | देवेभ्यस्त्वा सधमादमस्मभ्यं तवा सधमादम ||
meaning_en: O Food, from thee as such have we drawn forth with lauds, like cows, our sacrificial gifts, From thee who banquetest with Gods, from thee who banquetest with us.

sa_devanagari: तवमग्ने तवष्टा विधते सुवीर्यं तव गनावो मित्रमहः सजात्यम | तवमाशुहेमा ररिषे सवश्व्यं तवं नरां शर्धो असि पुरूवसुः ||
meaning_en: Thou givest strength, as Tvaṣṭar, to the worshipper: thou wielding Mitra's power hast kinship with the Dames. Thou, urging thy fleet coursers, givest noble steeds: a host of heroes art thou with great store of wealth.

sa_devanagari: तवमग्ने दरविणोदा अरंक्र्ते तवं देवः सविता रत्नधासि | तवं भगो नर्पते वस्व ईशिषे तवं पायुर्दमे यस्तेऽविधत ||
meaning_en: Giver of wealth art thou to him who honours thee; thou art God Savitar, granter of precious things. As Bhaga, Lord of men! thou rulest over wealth, and guardest in his house him who hath served thee well.

sa_devanagari: तवमग्ने अदितिर्देव दाशुषे तवं होत्रा भारती वर्धसेगिरा | तवमिळा षतहिमासि दक्षसे तवं वर्त्रहा वसुपते सरस्वती ||
meaning_en: Thou, God, art Aditi to him who offers gifts: thou, Hotrā, Bhāratī, art strengthened by the song. Thou art the hundred-wintered Iḷā to give strength, Lord of Wealth! Vṛtra-slayer and Sarasvatī.

sa_devanagari: तवमग्ने सुभ्र्त उत्तमं वयस्तव सपार्हे वर्ण आ सन्द्र्शि शरियः | तवं वाजः परतरणो बर्हन्नसि तवं रयिर्बहुलो विश्वतस पर्थुः ||
meaning_en: Thou, Agni, cherished well, art highest vital power; in thy delightful hue are glories visible. Thou art the lofty might that furthers each design: thou art wealth manifold, diffused on every side.

sa_devanagari: तवामग्न आदित्यास आस्यं तवां जिह्वां शुचयश्चक्रिरेकवे | तवां रातिषाचो अध्वरेषु सश्चिरे तवे देवा हविरदन्त्याहुतम ||
meaning_en: Thee, Agni, have the Ādityas taken as their mouth; the Bright Ones have made thee, O Sage, to be their tongue. They who love offerings cling to thee at solemn rites: by thee the Gods devour the duly offered food.

sa_devanagari: तवे अग्ने विश्वे अन्र्तासो अद्रुह आसा देवा हविरदन्त्याहुतम | तवया मर्तासः सवदन्त आसुतिं तवं गर्भो वीरुधां जज्ञिषे शुचिः ||
meaning_en: By thee, O Agni, all the Immortal guileless Gods cat with thy mouth the oblation that is offered them. By thee do mortal men give sweetness to their drink. Bright art thou born, the embryo of the plants of earth.

sa_devanagari: तवं तान सं च परति चासि मज्मनाग्ने सुजात पर च देवरिच्यसे | पर्क्षो यदत्र महिना वि ते भुवदनु दयावाप्र्थिवी रोदसी उभे ||
meaning_en: With these thou art united, Agni; yea thou, God of noble birth, surpassest them in majesty, Which, through the power of good, here spreads abroad from thee, diffused through both the worlds, throughout the earth and heaven.

sa_devanagari: ये सतोत्र्भ्यो गोग्रामश्वपेशसमग्ने रातिमुपस्र्जन्ति सूरयः | अस्माञ्च तांश्च पर हि नेषि वस्य आ बर्हद वदेम विदथे सुवीराः ||
meaning_en: The princely worshippers who send to those who sing thy praise, O Agni, guerdon graced with kine and steeds,— Lead thou both these and us forward to higher bliss. With brave men in the assembly may we speak aloud.

sa_devanagari: तवं नो असि भारताग्ने वशाभिरुक्षभिः | अष्टापदीभिराहुतः ||
meaning_en: Ours art thou, Agni, Bhārata, honoured by us with barren cows, With bullocks and with kine in calf

sa_devanagari: सनेम ये त ऊतिभिस्तरन्तो विश्वा सप्र्ध आर्येण दस्यून | अस्मभ्यं तत तवाष्ट्रं विश्वरूपमरन्धयः साख्यस्य तरिताय ||
meaning_en: May we gain wealth, subduing with thy succour and with the Ārya, all our foes, the Dasyus. Our gain was that to Tṛta of our party thou gavest up Tvaṣṭar's son Viśvarūpa.

sa_devanagari: अस्य सुवानस्य मन्दिनस्त्रितस्य नयर्बुदं वाव्र्धानो अस्तः | अवर्तयत सूर्यो न चक्रं भिनद वलमिन्द्रो अङगिरस्वान ||
meaning_en: He cast down Arbuda what time his vigour was strengthened by libations poured by Tṛta. Indra sent forth his whirling wheel like Sūrya, and aided by the Aṅgirases rent Vala.

sa_devanagari: नूनं सा ते परति वरं जरित्रे दुहीयदिन्द्र दक्षिणा मघोनी | शिक्षा सतोत्र्भ्यो माति धग भगो नो बर्हद वदेम व. स. ||
meaning_en: Now let that wealthy Cow of thine, O Indra, yield in return a boon to him who lauds thee. Give to thy praisers: let not fortune fail us. Loud may we speak, with brave men, in the assembly.

sa_devanagari: अध्वर्यवो यः शतं शम्बरस्य पुरो बिभेदाश्मनेव पूर्वीः | यो वर्चिनः शतमिन्द्रः सहस्रमपावपद भरतासोममस्मै ||
meaning_en: Ye ministers, to him who as with thunder demolished Śambara's hundred ancient castles; Who cast down Varcin's sons, a hundred thousand,—to him, to Indra, offer ye the Soma.

sa_devanagari: अस्मभ्यं तद ... ||
meaning_en: Prepare thyself to grant us that great booty, O Vasu, for abundant is thy treasure. Gather up wondrous wealth, O Indra, daily. Loud may we speak, with heroes, in assembly.

sa_devanagari: अस्य मन्दानो मध्वो वज्रहस्तो.अहिमिन्द्रो अर्णोव्र्तं वि वर्श्चत | पर यद वयो स सवसराण्यछा परयांसि च नदीनां चक्रमन्त ||
meaning_en: Cheered by this meath Indra, whose hand wields thunder, rent piecemeal Ahi who barred up the waters, So that the quickening currents of the rivers flowed forth like birds unto their resting-places.

sa_devanagari: बरह्मणस पते तवमस्य यन्ता सूक्तस्य बोधि तनयं च जिन्व | विश्वं तद भद्रं यदवन्ति देवा बर्हद वदेम ... ||
meaning_en: O Brahmaṇaspati, be thou controller of this our hymn and prosper thou our children. All that the Gods regard with love is blessed. Loud may we speak, with heroes, in assembly.

sa_devanagari: तस्मा इद विश्वे धुनयन्त सिन्धवो.अछिद्रा शर्म दधिरे पुरूणि | देवानां सुम्ने सुभगः स एधते यं-यं ... ||
meaning_en: Missing Line From Griffith.

sa_devanagari: वि मच्छ्रथाय रशनामिवाग रध्याम ते वरुण खां रतस्य | मा तन्तुश्छेदि वयतो धियं मे मा मात्रा शार्यपसः पुर रतोः ||
meaning_en: He made them flow, the Āditya, the Sustainer: the rivers run by Varuṇa's commandment. These feel no weariness, nor cease from flowing: swift have they flown like birds in air around us.

sa_devanagari: यो मे राजन युज्यो वा सखा वा सवप्ने भयं भीरवे मह्यमाह | सतेनो वा यो दिप्सति नो वर्को वा तवं तस्माद वरुणपाह्यस्मान ||
meaning_en: Move far from me what sins I have committed: let me not suffer, King, for guilt of others. Full many a morn remains to dawn upon us: in these, O Varuṇa, while we live direct us.

sa_devanagari: परेतां यज्ञस्य शम्भुवा युवामिदा वर्णीमहे | अग्निंच हव्यवाहनम ||
meaning_en: Ye who bless sacrifice, go forth, for verily we choose you both, And Agni who conveys our gifts.

sa_devanagari: दयावा नः पर्थिवी इमं सिध्रमद्य दिविस्प्र्शम | यज्णन्देवेषु यछताम ||
meaning_en: This our effectual sacrifice, reaching the sky, shall Heaven and Earth Present unto the Gods to-day.

sa_devanagari: आ वामुपस्थमद्रुहा देवाः सीदन्तु यज्ञियाः | इहाद्यसोमपीतये ||
meaning_en: In both your laps, ye guileless Ones, the Holy Gods shall sit them down To-day to drink the Soma here.

sa_devanagari: एता ते अग्ने जनिमा सनानि पर पूर्व्याय नूतनानि वोचम | महान्ति वर्ष्णे सवना कर्तेमा जन्मञ-जन्मन निहितो जातवेदाः ||
meaning_en: To thee who art of old these songs, O Agni, have I declared, the ancient and the later. These great libations to the Strong are offiered: in every birth is Jātavedas stablished.

sa_devanagari: वैश्वानर तव धामान्या चके येभिः सवर्विदभवो विचक्षण | जात आप्र्णो भुवनानि रोदसी अग्ने ता विस्वा परिभूरसि तमना ||
meaning_en: I celebrate thy glories, O Vaiśvānara, wherewith thou, O farsighted God, has found the light. Thou filledst at thy birth both worlds, the earth and heaven: all this, O Agni, hast thou compassed of thyself.

sa_devanagari: अमन्थिष्टां भारता रेवदग्निं देवश्रवा देववातः सुदक्षम | अग्ने वि पश्य बर्हताभि रायेषां नो नेता भवतादनु दयून ||
meaning_en: Both Bharatas, Devaśravas, Devāvata, have strongly rubbed to life effectual Agni. O Agni, look thou forth with ample riches: be, every day, bearer of food to feed us.

sa_devanagari: अग्ने दा दाशुषे रयिं वीरवन्तं परीणसम | शिशीहि नः सूनुमतः ||
meaning_en: Missing Line From Griffith.

sa_devanagari: वर्षणं तवा वयं वर्षन वर्षणः समिधीमहि | अग्ने दीद्यतं बर्हत ||
meaning_en: Missing line from Griffith.

sa_devanagari: मिहः पावकाः परतता अभूवन सवस्ति नः पिप्र्हि पारमासाम | इन्द्र तवं रथिरः पाहि नो रिषो मक्षू-मक्षू कर्णुहि गोजितो नः ||
meaning_en: Far forth are spread the purifying waters convey thou us across them unto safety. Save us, our Charioteer, from harm, O Indra, soon, very soon, make us win spoil of cattle.

sa_devanagari: आ तू भर माकिरेतत परि षठाद विद्मा हि तवा वसुपतिं वसूनाम | इन्द्र यत ते माहिनं दत्रमस्त्यस्मभ्यं तद धर्यश्व पर यन्धि ||
meaning_en: Then bring thou hither, and let none prevent it: we know thee well, the Lord of wealth and treasure. That splendid gift which is thine own, O Indra, vouchsafe to us, Lord of the Tawny Coursers.

sa_devanagari: अभि दयुम्नानि वनिन इन्द्रं सचन्ते अक्षिता | पीत्वी सोमस्य वाव्र्धे ||
meaning_en: To Indra go the treasures of the worshipper, which never fail: He drinks the Soma and is strong

sa_devanagari: इन्द्रो हर्यन्तमर्जुनं वज्रं शुक्रैरभीव्र्तम | अपाव्र्णोद धरिभिरद्रिभिः सुतमुद गा हरिभिराजत ||
meaning_en: The bright, the well-loved thunderbolt, girt with the bright, Indra disclosed, Disclosed the Soma juice pressed out by tawny stones, with tawny steeds drave forth the kine.

sa_devanagari: इन्द्र मरुत्व इह पाहि सोमं यथा शार्याते अपिबः सुतस्य | तव परणीती तव शूर शर्मन्ना विवासन्ति कवयःसुयज्ञाः ||
meaning_en: Here, Indra, drink thou Soma with the Maruts, as thou didst drink the juice beside Śāryāta. Under thy guidance, in thy keeping, Hero, the singers serve, skilled in fair sacrifices.

sa_devanagari: अप्तूर्ये मरुत आपिरेषो.अमन्दन्निन्द्रमनु दातिवाराः | तेभिः साकं पिबतु वर्त्रखादः सुतं सोमं दाशुषः सवे सधस्थे ||
meaning_en: He was your comrade in your zeal, O Maruts: they, rich in noble gifts, rejoiced in Indra. With them together let the Vṛtra-slayer drink in his home the worshipper's libation.

sa_devanagari: अयमस्मान वनस्पतिर्मा च हा मा च रीरिषत | सवस्त्याग्र्हेभ्य आवसा आ विमोचनात ||
meaning_en: Let not this sovran of the wood leave us forlorn or injure us. Safe may we be until we reach our homes and rest us and unyoke.

sa_devanagari: शर्ण्वन्तु नो वर्षणः पर्वतासो धरुवक्षेमास इळया मदन्तः | आदित्यैर्नो अदितिः शर्णोतु यछन्तु नो मरुतः शर्मभद्रम ||
meaning_en: Hear us the mouatains which distil the rain-drops, and, resting firm, rejoice in freshening moisture. May Aditi with the Ādityas hear us, and Maruts grant us their auspicious shelter.

sa_devanagari: मही समैरच्चम्वा समीची उभे ते अस्य वसुना नय्र्ष्टे | शर्ण्वे वीरो विन्दमानो वसूनि म... ||
meaning_en: The two great meeting Bowls hath he united: each of the Pair is laden with histreasure. The Hero is renowned for gathering riches. Great is the Gods’ supreme and sole dominion.

sa_devanagari: न ता मिनन्ति मायिनो न धीरा वरता देवानां परथमा धरुवाणि | न रोदसी अद्रुहा वेद्याभिर्न पर्वता निनमे तस्थिवांसः ||
meaning_en: NOT men of magic skill, not men of wisdom impair the Gods’ first steadfast ordinances. Ne’er may the earth and heaven which know not malice, nor the fixed hills, be bowed by sage devices.

sa_devanagari: उत तया यजता हरी कुमारात साहदेव्यात | परयता सद्य आ ददे ||
meaning_en: And truly those two noble bays I straightway took when offered me, From Sahadeva's princely son.

sa_devanagari: तं युवं देवाव अश्विना कुमारं साहदेव्यम | दीर्घायुषं कर्णोतन ||
meaning_en: Cause him the youthful prince, the son of Sahadeva, to enjoy Long life, O Aśvins, O ye Gods.

sa_devanagari: कविर न निण्यं विदथानि साधन वर्षा यत सेकं विपिपानो अर्चात | दिव इत्था जीजनत सप्त कारून अह्ना चिच चक्रुर वयुना गर्णन्तः ||
meaning_en: When the Bull, quaffing, praises our libation, as a sage paying holy rites in secret, Seven singers here from heaven hath he begotten, who e’en by day have wrought their works while singing.

sa_devanagari: एवेद इन्द्राय वर्षभाय वर्ष्णे बरह्माकर्म भर्गवो न रथम | नू चिद यथा नः सख्या वियोषद असन न उग्रो ऽविता तनूपाः ||
meaning_en: Now, as the Bhṛgus wrought a car, for Indra the Strong, the Mighty, we our prayer have fashioned, That he may, ne’er withdraw from us his friendship, but be our bodies' guard and strong defender.

sa_devanagari: न घा स माम अप जोषं जभाराभीम आस तवक्षसा वीर्येण | ईर्मा पुरंधिर अजहाद अरातीर उत वातां अतरच छूशुवानः ||
meaning_en: Not at his own free pleasure did he bear me: he conquered with his strength and manly courage. Straightway the Bold One left the fiends behind him and passed the winds as he grew yet more mighty.

sa_devanagari: शतम अश्मन्मयीनाम पुराम इन्द्रो वय आस्यत | दिवोदासाय दाशुषे ||
meaning_en: For Divodāsa, him who brought oblations, Indra overthrew A hundred fortresses of stone.

sa_devanagari: उत नूनं यद इन्द्रियं करिष्या इन्द्र पौंस्यम | अद्या नकिष टद आ मिनत ||
meaning_en: Indra, whatever deed of might thou hast this day to execute, None be there now to hinder it.

sa_devanagari: वामं-वामं त आदुरे देवो ददात्व अर्यमा | वामम पूषा वामम भगो वामं देवः करूळती ||
meaning_en: O Watchful One, may Aryaman the God give thee all goodly things. May Pūṣan, Bhaga, and the God Karūḷatī give all things fair.
